matter.title: 1031 like-kind exchange: a 6-unit apartment building at 22 Sherman Street for a self-storage facility in Sanford — Grace C. Roe

jurisdiction.court_type: Maine Registry of Deeds / qualified intermediary escrow

matter.summary: Grace C. Roe is selling a 6-unit apartment building at 22 Sherman Street in Portland for $669985 and, through a delayed exchange identifying under the three-property rule, acquiring a self-storage facility in Sanford for $1817628 to defer gain under IRC § 1031, with replacement to close by 2026-08-18.

fact_pattern.summary: Grace C. Roe is selling a 6-unit apartment building at 22 Sherman Street in Portland for $669985 and, through a delayed exchange identifying under the three-property rule, acquiring a self-storage facility in Sanford for $1817628 to defer gain under IRC § 1031, with replacement to close by 2026-08-18.
fact_pattern.narrative:
Grace C. Roe owns a 6-unit apartment building at 22 Sherman Street in Portland, Sagadahoc County (adjusted basis about $739078, debt about $431926) and is selling it to Maria Q. Fairbanks for $669985. To defer the gain, the sale is structured as a delayed exchange identifying under the three-property rule under IRC § 1031, with a qualified intermediary holding the net proceeds so the exchangor never has actual or constructive receipt.

The relinquished leg closes about 2026-02-19. The exchangor must identify replacement property in writing by the 45-day deadline of 2026-04-05 under the 200% fair-market-value rule, and must close on it by the 180-day deadline of 2026-08-18. The target is a self-storage facility in Sanford, purchased from Ingrid B. Kingsley for $1817628 with about $976437 of new debt, expected to close 2026-07-12.

To fully defer, the exchangor must reinvest all net proceeds and match or exceed the relinquished value and debt — watching for cash boot if the replacement price is below the relinquished price. Diligence confirms an unrelated seller (no related-party issue). Counsel Omar W. Loe of Greenhill Law Offices and the CPA are coordinating the QI exchange documents, both deeds, and the Form 8824 reporting.
undisputed_facts: Grace C. Roe is selling a 6-unit apartment building at 22 Sherman Street for $669985 as part of a delayed exchange identifying under the three-property rule.
A qualified intermediary is holding the proceeds; the exchangor avoids constructive receipt.
Identification is due by 2026-04-05 and replacement closing by 2026-08-18.
disputed_facts: Whether the $1817628 replacement and $976437 debt fully avoid cash boot if the replacement price is below the relinquished price.
Whether the identification under the 200% fair-market-value rule is sufficient if the primary target falls through.
The deferred gain and carryover basis computation for Form 8824.

third_parties.name: Cobblestone Partners Corp.
third_parties.relevance: Holds exchange proceeds and documents the 1031 exchange.

third_parties.name: Harbor Studios Inc.
third_parties.relevance: Closes both legs and directs proceeds through the QI.

third_parties.name: Lighthouse Trust Company
third_parties.relevance: Finances the replacement property to match or exceed relinquished debt.

third_parties.name: Beatrice S. Danforth
third_parties.relevance: Computes deferred gain, basis, and boot, and reports Form 8824.

exchanges.question: What are you selling?
exchanges.answer: a 6-unit apartment building at 22 Sherman Street in Portland for $669985.

exchanges.question: Why the exchange?
exchanges.answer: To defer the gain under § 1031 through a delayed exchange identifying under the three-property rule; my basis is only about $739078.

exchanges.question: What are you buying?
exchanges.answer: a self-storage facility in Sanford for $1817628 from Ingrid B. Kingsley.

exchanges.question: Do you understand the clock?
exchanges.answer: Yes — identify by 2026-04-05 and close by 2026-08-18, using the 200% fair-market-value rule.

exchanges.question: Any boot exposure?
exchanges.answer: We're watching for cash boot if the replacement price is below the relinquished price and matching value and debt.

exchanges.question: Who holds the money?
exchanges.answer: The qualified intermediary — I can't touch the proceeds or I blow the exchange.

goals: Fully defer gain under IRC § 1031 by reinvesting all proceeds and matching value and debt.
Close the replacement (a self-storage facility in Sanford) by 2026-07-12.
priorities: Strict compliance with the QI, identification, and timing rules.
Avoiding cash boot if the replacement price is below the relinquished price.
desired_outcomes: A completed exchange with no or minimal boot and a clean Form 8824.
non_starters: Taking actual or constructive receipt of the proceeds.

constraints.budget: Reinvest all $669985 of proceeds; new debt about $976437.

issues.issue: Qualifying like-kind exchange and non-receipt of proceeds
issues.governing_law: IRC § 1031; Treas. Reg. § 1.1031(k)-1 (qualified intermediary safe harbor)
sub_questions: Does the exchangor avoid constructive receipt?

issues.issue: Maine real estate transfer tax on each leg
sub_questions: Is the transfer tax due on both the relinquished and replacement deeds?
Is there cash or mortgage boot given cash boot if the replacement price is below the relinquished price?
Is identification timely and compliant with the 200% fair-market-value rule?

communications.subject: 1031 setup — a 6-unit apartment building at 22 Sherman Street
communications.summary: CPA confirms exchange feasibility and the deferral targets.
communications.body: With a $669985 sale price against roughly $739078 basis, a 6-unit apartment building at 22 Sherman Street has significant built-in gain. To fully defer under § 1031 you must reinvest all net proceeds and acquire replacement property of equal or greater value ($1817628 works) while replacing at least $431926 of debt. Watch for cash boot if the replacement price is below the relinquished price. We must engage a qualified intermediary holding the net proceeds so the exchangor never has actual or constructive receipt before the relinquished closing on 2026-02-19.

communications.subject: Exchange funds received — a 6-unit apartment building at 22 Sherman Street
communications.summary: QI confirms receipt of proceeds and states the 45/180-day deadlines.
communications.body: We have received the net proceeds from the sale of a 6-unit apartment building at 22 Sherman Street to Maria Q. Fairbanks and are holding them under the exchange agreement so you have no actual or constructive receipt. Your 45-day identification deadline is 2026-04-05 and your 180-day exchange deadline is 2026-08-18. Identify replacement property in writing under the 200% fair-market-value rule.

communications.subject: 45-day identification — replacement property
communications.summary: Exchangor delivers the written identification of replacement property.
communications.body: Under the 200% fair-market-value rule, I identify a self-storage facility in Sanford in Sanford (purchase price $1817628) as my primary replacement property, plus one backup as permitted. Please acknowledge receipt within the 45-day window ending 2026-04-05. We expect to close the replacement around 2026-07-12.

communications.subject: Replacement closing — a self-storage facility in Sanford
communications.summary: Closing agent confirms the replacement leg and QI funds direction.
communications.body: The replacement closing for a self-storage facility in Sanford is set for 2026-07-12, within the 2026-08-18 deadline. The Qualified Intermediary will wire the exchange funds directly to this closing toward the $1817628 price, with about $976437 in new financing from the lender. We will record the warranty deed and the RETTD; the CPA will report the exchange on Form 8824.

communications.subject: Exchange complete — Form 8824 summary
communications.summary: CPA reports the completed exchange, deferred gain, and carryover basis.
communications.body: Your § 1031 exchange is complete within the 180-day period. By reinvesting the full $669985 of proceeds into a self-storage facility in Sanford at $1817628 and replacing debt, you deferred substantially all of the gain over your $739078 basis, subject to cash boot if the replacement price is below the relinquished price. We will report the exchange on Form 8824 and carry your basis forward into the replacement property. an unrelated seller (no related-party issue) was confirmed, so no related-party holding issue applies.

facts.relinquished_property: a 6-unit apartment building at 22 Sherman Street
facts.exchange_type: a delayed exchange identifying under the three-property rule
facts.boot_risk: cash boot if the replacement price is below the relinquished price
facts.qi_role: a qualified intermediary holding the net proceeds so the exchangor never has actual or constructive receipt
